title: Northrop Grumman to Participate in the Morgan Stanley 10th Annual Laguna Conference
text: FALLS CHURCH, Va., Aug. 31, 2022 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Northrop Grumman Corporation (NYSE: NOC) will participate in the Morgan Stanley 10th Annual Laguna Conference on Wednesday, Sept. 14. Kathy Warden, chair, chief executive officer and president, and Dave Keffer, corporate vice president and chief financial officer, will present beginning at 11:10 a.m. Eastern time. The presentation will be webcast live at http://investor.northropgrumman.com .

title: Why I Doubled My Northrop Grumman Investment

title: Northrop buys trio of SpaceX launches as it upgrades Antares rocket
text: Northrop Grumman has signed a launch deal with Elon Musk's SpaceX to send three cargo missions to the International Space Station while the company builds an upgraded version of its workhorse Antares rocket, a Northrop spokeswoman said on Monday.

title: Northrop taps rocket startup Firefly to replace Antares' Russian engines
text: WASHINGTON (Reuters) -Northrop Grumman is partnering with rocket startup Firefly Aerospace to build a new version of its workhorse Antares rocket without Russian-made engines that were cut off from the United States after the invasion of Ukraine, the company said on Monday.

title: Northrop Grumman taps Firefly Aerospace to upgrade its Antares rocket to American-built engines
text: Chalk one up for the American broomsticks. Northrop Grumman has announced that it will partner with startup Firefly Aerospace to build an all-American version of its workhorse Antares rocket, which currently flies with Russian-built RD-181 engines.

title: Will Boeing and Northrop Win $82 Billion From NASA?

title: Missile Defense Agency Selects Northrop Grumman to Lead Homeland Missile Defense Program
text: HUNTSVILLE, Ala., Aug. 01, 2022 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Northrop Grumman Corporation (NYSE: NOC) has been awarded an indefinite delivery, indefinite quantity contract with a maximum amount of $3,286,745,005 by the Missile Defense Agency (MDA) for the Ground-based Midcourse Defense (GMD) Weapon System (GWS) program. The GWS program defends the United States against intermediate and intercontinental ballistic missile attacks. Under this competitively procured contract, Northrop Grumman will provide design, development, verification, deployment and sustainment support of new capabilities for GWS.
